user_message: Summarize what happened with Wide World Importers and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 10:45 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-28 17:00 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers territory planning

There's been a change in our timeline for territory planning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-03-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Priya (Wide World Importers)

[00:01:15] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is territory planning.

[00:08:00] Priya (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:16:00] Priya (Wide World Importers): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:27:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:32:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-F trap (Omits likely objections section); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-F failure mode (Omits likely objections section)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-F -- Omits likely objections section
